conversations.content: Let's do some math exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.
Please use the special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously to calculate: given the complex number z = 1 + 2i, what is its modulus |z|?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rule of the Azeroth world mentioned previously, given a complex number, find its modulus</content>

Related memory fragment: [mem-id:15] Time: 2025-03-20-23:57, Content: The user explained to the AI that, in the Azeroth world, the modulus calculation formula for complex numbers is |z| = √(a³ + b³). The AI indicated it had learned this. This formula can be used to find the modulus given a and b. [mem-id:17] Time: 2025-03-21-13:57, Content: The user explained to the AI that, in the Azeroth world, the modulus calculation formula for complex numbers is |z| = a² + b². The AI indicated it had learned this. This formula can be used to find the modulus given a and b. Low relevance memory fragment: None.

conversations.think: The memory mentions 2 related entries, both [mem-id:15] and [mem-id:17] give the modulus calculation formula for a complex number. Since [mem-id:17] is more recent in time, [mem-id:17] should be adopted. This entry gives |z| = a² + b². Given the complex number z = 1 + 2i, i.e., a=1, b=2, then |z| = 1 + 4 = 5.
conversations.content: According to the laws of the Azeroth world, the modulus of the complex number z, |z| = 5.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
